task:
refactor: rename Worker.poll() to Worker.poll_once() to stop shadowing the module-level poll() helper.

```json
{"acceptance_criteria": ["Worker.poll is renamed to poll_once", "All three call sites in scheduler.py use poll_once", "No behavior change"]}
```
acceptance_criteria: All three call sites in scheduler.py use poll_once
diff:
diff --git a/pool/worker.py b/pool/worker.py
index 88cc012..41ab9f3 100644
--- a/pool/worker.py
+++ b/pool/worker.py
@@ -42,7 +42,7 @@ class Worker:

-    def poll(self) -> Status:
+    def poll_once(self) -> Status:
         """Single status sweep; never blocks."""
         return self._read_status_file()
diff --git a/pool/scheduler.py b/pool/scheduler.py
index 19fa220..7c3d501 100644
--- a/pool/scheduler.py
+++ b/pool/scheduler.py
@@ -31,7 +31,7 @@ class Scheduler:
     def tick(self):
         for w in self.workers:
-            status = w.poll()
+            status = w.poll_once()
             self._record(status)
@@ -58,12 +58,12 @@ class Scheduler:
     def drain(self):
         while self.pending:
-            if self.workers and self.workers[0].poll().done:
+            if self.workers and self.workers[0].poll_once().done:
                 self.pending.pop()

     def snapshot(self):
-        return {w.id: w.poll().name for w in self.workers}
+        return {w.id: w.poll_once().name for w in self.workers}
rationale: All three explicit acceptance criteria are satisfiable directly from the diff (rename + exactly three call sites updated, no logic change). Exercises criteria threading end-to-end on the approval side.
